recommendations.problem: Runtime bug in syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0 causes restrictedHeaders to be ignored

solution.command:
# Via AWS Console:
# 1. Navigate to CloudWatch Synthetics console
# 2. Select your canary
# 3. Click on a recent run > "HTTP steps" tab
# 4. Check if restricted headers (e.g., Authorization) appear in plain text

# Via AWS CLI:
aws synthetics get-canary \
  --name <canary-name> \
  --region <canary-region>
solution.description: Verify headers are not being redacted in canary reports
solution.expected_outcome: Confirms canary is on syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0 and headers are unredacted

solution.command:
# Via AWS Console:
# 1. Navigate to CloudWatch Synthetics console
# 2. Select your canary
# 3. Click "Actions" > "Edit"
# 4. Update runtime version to syn-nodejs-puppeteer-12.0 or later
# 5. Save changes

# Via AWS CLI:
aws synthetics update-canary \
  --name <canary-name> \
  --region <canary-region> \
  --runtime-version syn-nodejs-puppeteer-12.0
solution.description: Update runtime to the version where the restrictedHeaders fix is included
solution.expected_outcome: Canary updated to runtime with restrictedHeaders fix

solution.command:
# Via AWS Console:
# 1. Navigate to CloudWatch Synthetics console
# 2. Select your canary and trigger a run
# 3. Click on the new run > "HTTP steps" tab
# 4. Verify restricted headers show as "REDACTED"
solution.description: Confirm restricted headers no longer appear in reports
solution.expected_outcome: Restricted headers appear as REDACTED in HTTP step reports
solution.step: Verify headers are redacted after upgrade

recommendations.title: Upgrade to syn-nodejs-puppeteer-12.0 or later

root_cause: A regression in syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0 causes the executeHttpStep function to skip the header redaction logic when building HTTPRequestReports. The restrictedHeaders array is accepted but never applied during report generation, so sensitive headers (e.g., Authorization, X-Api-Key) appear in plain text in step logs and HAR artifacts. This is a code-path regression from syn-nodejs-puppeteer-10.0, fixed in syn-nodejs-puppeteer-12.0.
symptoms: Headers specified in restrictedHeaders are not redacted in HTTPRequestReports
Sensitive headers visible in HTTPSteps logs and reports
Same code works correctly on syn-nodejs-puppeteer-10.0 but not 11.0
title: syn-nodejs-puppeteer-11.0 ignoring 'restrictedHeaders' in executeHttpStep
